patterns_recognized: D-grade dual-signal-against pattern went 0-for-4 on 5/25 (all four D-grade picks won model direction, e.g. MIA won 8-2, CIN won 7-2, ARI won 6-2, TB/BAL won 9-7); cumulative directional failure rate has fallen below 55% — no longer a high-confidence direction reversal, treat as weak negative signal only.

Claude DOWNGRADEs triggered by stress flags (confidence_downgrade, bullpen_fatigue, F1* thin) went 3-of-4 wrong directionally on 5/25 (MIN@CHW, NYY@KC, PHI@SD all models correct); these flags should suppress stake size but not flip direction — avoid using as standalone DOWNGRADE triggers.

HARD CAP 7 near-zero firings (+0.014 on 5/25 WSH@CLE, +0.0014 on 5/20 HOU@MIN) provided insufficient EV protection on PLATINUM-level picks; CLE lost 2-10 despite minimal gap — WSH@CLE repeat matchup today warrants extra caution on CLE despite positive internal signals.

BvP OWNER entries without corroborating D-grade or negative-edge signals failed as standalone DOWNGRADE triggers on 5/25 (PHI@SD: Tatis/Machado OWNERs used to downgrade PHI → PHI won 3-0); require at minimum one corroborating structural signal before using OWNER BvP to override F-signal conviction.

HARD CAP 1 (negative edge) has blocked stake on ~50% winning/~50% losing directional calls across the full archive — market information superiority is EV-protective and directionally neutral; never relax based on F-signal conviction alone (validated again by 5/25 STL@MIL/COL@LAD wins and WSH@CLE loss).

The D-grade dual-signal-against pattern has weakened recently (0-for-4 on 5/25) but the market gap here is genuine and large.
